rulings.Case Title: TARICK SHAMIYEH VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO BIFURCATE/SEVER / Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 12, 2026, line 1. PETITIONER TARICK SHAMIYEH'S Motion To Bifurcate Proceedings. Petitioner Tarick Shamiyeh's motion to bifurcate proceedings is granted in large part. Shamiyeh's request for judicial notice is granted in full. This writ proceeding will be divided into two phases. In the first phase the parties will address the applicable standard of review for Shamiyeh's administrative appeal and whether it was legal error for the ALJ to impose a burden of proof on Shamiyeh in the administrative appeal. All other issues, including whether Shamiyeh had any right to introduce non-record evidence beyond what is permitted by Rule 6B of the Rules for Administrative Appeal, will be addressed in the second phase, if any. Judicial economy is served by the foregoing bifurcation. The first phase issues are discrete and easily segregable from the second phase issues and necessarily must be decided prior to the second phase issues. The decision on those issues are possibly outcome-determinative. The first phase issues are subject of serious dispute, possibly novel, and involve pure issues of law. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: YOLANDA JONES ET AL VS. GENERAL MOTORS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473(B) And Reinstate Case; Declaration Of George Hill
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 12, 2026, line 3. PLAINTIFFS YOLANDA JONES, LAMONT JOHNSON'S Motion To Set Aside Dismissal Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473(B) And Reinstate Case. Plaintiffs' motion to set aside dismissal is denied. Defendant General Motors' untimely opposition papers were not considered. Plaintiffs fail to offer any facts, law or circumstances that they did not or could not raise at the time this case was dismissed. Plaintiffs' failure to be ready for a prove-up hearing is not the kind of mistake that is remediable by Code of Civil Procedure 437(b) nor do plaintiffs cite any authority suggesting otherwise. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: D&M CARRIERS, LLC DBA FREYMILLER VS. PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 12, 2026, line 5. DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint filed by D&M Carriers, LLC is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. If it can do so in good faith and in accordance with applicable law and the facts as it understands them, D&M has leave to file a second amended complaint setting forth one or more viable claims against Progressive Casualty Insurance Company. The sole cause of action alleged in the first amended complaint assumes that an insurance policy issued by Progressive Casualty Insurance Company provided defense and indemnity coverage for the November 30, 2023 accident yet D&M's opposition to this demurrer argues that whether Progressive Casualty Insurance Company issued such a policy "misses the point." Under these unusual circumstances, the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint is contradicted by the D'&M's own filing and fails to state a claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. DOORDASH, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Materials Lodged In Connection With Defendant Doordash, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 12, 2026, line 6. PLAINTIFF UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'S Motion To Seal Materials Lodged In Connection With Defendant Doordash, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration. Uber's motion to seal is denied. Uber has not shown that it has an overriding interest in the information that it seeks to have sealed that overcomes the right of public access or that a substantial probability exists that any such overriding interest would be prejudiced if this motion is not granted. The facts that Uber designated the information as confidential and that there is no opposition to this motion are not sufficient to support a sealing order. (CRC 2.551(a)). The declarations supporting the motion are largely conclusory and fail to support a sealing order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE ET AL VS. PRC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for January 12, 2026, line 8. DEFENDANT ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant St. Anthony Foundation's demurrer to the ninth cause of action for negligent hiring and supervision and the tenth cause of action for respondeat superior alleged against it by plaintiff Jane Doe is overruled as to the ninth cause of action and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the tenth cause of action. As to both causes of action St. Anthony argues that its demurrer should be sustained because of misjoinder of the parties and failure to state a claim. St. Anthony's misjoinder argument lacks merit as to both causes of action since both are based on a single incident of alleged rape that Jane Doe alleges all defendants are liable for due to their negligent hiring and supervision. The ninth cause of action is adequately alleged based on the alleged ultimate facts that the alleged rapist had a history of violent criminal conduct and St. Anthony failed to implement proper prescreening and training, knew or should have known that the alleged rapist was violent and dangerous, and failed to supervise the alleged rapist. The tenth cause lacks sufficient ultimate facts to support respondeat superior liability. The allegation that the rape occurred while the alleged rapist was in the course and scope of his employment with St. Anthony is conclusory and unsupported by any ultimate facts. If she can do so in good faith, Jane Doe is granted leave to allege sufficient ultimate facts to support a viable claim for respondeat superior liability. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
